qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal and RCE via unvalidated module path in elixir-nodejs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes path traversal and RCE through unvalidated module path parameters in server.js (CWE-22). The advisory describes a race condition in the worker protocol causing stale response cross-user data leakage (CWE-362). The root causes, vulnerable code paths, and impact mechanisms are entirely different. The finding concerns server.js path.join() without boundary validation; the advisory concerns worker request-response correlation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a race condition in the elixir-nodejs worker protocol where lack of request-response correlation causes stale responses and cross-user data leakage (CWE-362, fixed in v3.1.4). The only qualifying finding describes a completely different vulnerability: path traversal and RCE through unvalidated module path parameters in server.js (CWE-22). The root causes (race condition in response handling vs. missing path validation in module loading), vulnerable code locations (worker response correlation logic vs. server.js path.join()), and security impacts (cross-user data leakage vs. arbitrary file read/RCE) are all distinct.
evidence: Advisory describes CWE-362 race condition in worker protocol response correlation
Advisory references issue #100 and PR #105 for the race condition fix
Finding describes CWE-22 path traversal via unvalidated module path in server.js
Finding's vulnerable code: server.js:26 path.join(), server.js:54-57 dynamic import/require
Advisory's vulnerable code: worker protocol response handling lacking request-response correlation
No overlap in root cause, vulnerable function, or control-flow path
